intro: Malvastrum mexicanum, also known as Mexican Mallow, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The bioactive constituents of Malvastrum mexicanum include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including gastrointestinal disorders and respiratory infections.
